title: rbac — helper functions
description: Two short helpers on top of the resolver + spatie facade. Both are convenience wrappers — nothing prevents callers from using `Gate::allows(...)` directly, which routes through the same resolver.

helpers.purpose: Short helper resolving via `PermissionResolver::resolve(auth()->user(), $permission, $resource)`. Returns bool.
behaviours: Simple: can('users.viewAny.tenant') → true if the caller holds that permission on the current tenant
With resource: can('invoices.view', $invoice) → consults grants on the invoice + falls back to RBAC
helpers.flow: 1) Load auth()->user() (short-circuits to false if null). 2) Load current tenant + application from RequestContext. 3) Delegate to PermissionResolver::resolve(). 4) Return the boolean.

helpers.purpose: Throws AuthorizationException on false. Equivalent to `Gate::authorize(...)` with the same routing semantics.
behaviours: Failure: throws Illuminate\Auth\Access\AuthorizationException with the permission name + resource type in the message
Missing permission in catalogue: still throws (default deny with warn-severity log)

helpers.purpose: Container-resolved PermissionResolver instance. Rare — most call sites use can() / authorize() / #[RequirePermission]. Reserve for services that need programmatic access to the resolver's diagnostics API (audit-permission-check paths).
behaviours: In tests: replaceable via App::instance() to swap in a test double

helpers.purpose: Normalise a Role model or role name to its canonical snake_case slug. Rarely needed — useful when integrating with third-party libraries that expect string role names.

notes: We do NOT re-declare Laravel's Gate helpers. `Gate::allows(...)` / `Gate::authorize(...)` route through our resolver via the AccessControlServiceProvider's `Gate::before()` hook, so existing Laravel code keeps working unchanged.

The `#[RequirePermission]` and `#[RequireRole]` framework attributes wrap `authorize()` at the middleware layer. Prefer the attribute over calling `authorize()` inline — attribute-first is design.md §2 D-A1's letter.
